tables.title: Initial Fees Summary
rows: Partial — $10K deposit non-refundable; balance refundable in 4 specified circumstances
table_notes: No initial franchise fee for conversion franchisees or relocating franchisees under relocation policy
ADQ may charge reduced non-refundable fee in certain situations (reopening closed location, certain captive venues, qualified multi-unit operators)
MultiTRA fee: $22,500 credited against each unit's initial franchise fee

tables.title: Other Fees
rows: 10th of each month for prior month
Gross Sales = total revenue from all product sales excluding sales taxes and gift card sale revenue. May be reduced for specialty Captive-venue locations.
10th of each month for prior month
ADQ determines percentage within range; 90 days advance notice of adjustment. May be reduced for specialty Captive-venue.
Increases $500 every 5 years starting Jan 1, 2030. Refundable less actual costs if ADQ refuses consent or exercises ROFR. MultiTRA not transferrable.
Unpaid fees on understated Gross Sales plus audit costs
If understatement ≥3%, franchisee pays all audit costs plus costs for additional audits within 2 years
2× royalty for last 12 months of active operations (or prorated formula)
Complex formula: if <12 months operation, 24× average monthly royalty; if <24 months remaining, months remaining × average monthly royalty. Applies only to ADQ-initiated default termination.
18% per annum or max contract rate permitted by law
On past due payments to ADQ
Must always have designated manager + 2 assistant managers trained
$150 for phase 1-2 cancel >14 days; $750 for phase 1-2 cancel ≤14 days; $1,000 for phase 3 cancel ≤21 days; $200 substitution fee <14 days before class
table_notes: All fees payable to ADQ or affiliates, uniformly imposed, nonrefundable (except as noted)
Conversion franchisees have special royalty phase-in schedules (see footnotes 2, 3)
Relocation franchisees carry over existing rates for years 1-5, mid-point for years 6-10, then standard
If late paying/reporting, ADQ may require weekly payment program
Franchisee must pay all business debts, liens and taxes promptly; ADQ may pay them and require reimbursement

tables.title: Estimated Initial Investment
rows: Travel and Living Expenses for Training
Building Plans, Design Intent Plans and Architectural Seal
Utility Deposits, Business Licenses, Govt Charges
Prior to opening and as incurred
table_notes: Does not include cost of land ($250K–$800K if purchasing)
MultiTRA trade area reservation fee not included
Two prototypical buildings: Next Gen Core 34 (1,938 sq ft, seats 34, lot min 25,830 sq ft) and Next Gen Core 46 (2,208 sq ft, seats 46, lot min 32,026 sq ft)
Leased facility: building cost $350K–$500K; owned facility: $600K–$980K (site work + building)
Equipment for owned Next Gen Core 34: $575K–$625K; Core 46: $625K–$650K
Lenders typically require 20% equity on leasehold improvements, 25% on equipment
Conversion costs may vary significantly depending on facility type/condition
Additional Funds high end assumes MultiTRA with Designated Supervisory Executive hire

tables.title: Supply Chain Revenue and Vendor Payments
rows: USCI authorized warehouse fee to USCI
USCI maximum average supply chain margin (2025)
table_notes: IDQ/ADQ margin commitment is permanent and binding on any future buyer of USCI
Manufactured frozen novelties under separate margin schedule, also 1.5% max in 2025
National vendor payments for marketing flow through NMF and are outside margin commitment scope

tables.title: Schedule A — Gross Sales (Qualifying DQ Grill & Chill Restaurants)
table_notes: Population: Newly constructed freestanding restaurants developed under ADQ new/ARD programs, opened Jan 2014–Dec 2023, operated all 12 months of noted year, franchisee-owned
Excludes: Texas DQ, conversions, fuel centers, non-traditional, territory operator subfranchised units
Excluded for not reporting all 12 months: 29 (2021), 33 (2022), 31 (2023), 19 (2024)

tables.title: Schedule B — Expense Categories and Manageable Profit
table_notes: Schedule B subset: restaurants that submitted usable P&Ls. Excluded from Schedule A count: 91 (2021), 101 (2022), 106 (2023), 285 (2024)
Manageable Profit = Gross Sales minus COGS, Labor, Controllables. BEFORE occupancy, insurance, royalties (4%), sales promotion fees (5-6%), legal/accounting/admin
Labor includes franchisee owner compensation paid as manager salary but not owner draws/dividends
2024 has dramatically smaller sample (55 vs. 321 in Schedule A) — 285 excluded for missing/problematic P&Ls

tables.title: DQ Grill & Chill / Brazier Direct-Licensed Systemwide Outlet Summary
table_notes: Excludes Texas DQ restaurants, subfranchised outlets, and other franchise programs in Item 1

tables.title: DQ G&C / Brazier Direct-Licensed Status of Franchised Outlets — Totals
table_notes: Conversions from another DQ concept or acquisitions of territory operator rights included in openings: 1 (2022), 4 (2023), 3 (2024)
Transfers to new owners: 108 (2022), 101 (2023), 113 (2024)

tables.title: Subfranchised Territory Operator Outlets — Systemwide Summary
table_notes: Territory operators not required to provide projected opening data to ADQ

tables.title: Texas DQ Restaurant — Systemwide Summary
table_notes: Texas DQ declining: 14 terminations vs 3 openings in 2024
ADQ acquired Texas territory operator subfranchisor rights in 1980 (over 900 subfranchised DQ restaurants)

tables.title: IDQ Consolidated Balance Sheet (Key Line Items)
table_notes: IDQ is consolidated parent of ADQ and all subsidiaries
Retained deficit indicates cumulative dividends exceed cumulative earnings — Berkshire extracts substantial dividends
Gift card liabilities ($102.7M) and committed advertising ($39.8M) are large pass-through liabilities

tables.title: IDQ Consolidated Income Statement
table_notes: Service fees (royalties) are dominant revenue source: 77% of total in 2024
SGA expenses: $115.6M (2024), 44.9% of revenue
Net income margin: 38.0% (2024)

tables.title: IDQ Consolidated Cash Flows
table_notes: Dividends to Berkshire: $110M (2024), $110M (2023), $85M (2022) — exceeds net income, funded partially from prior retained earnings
Cash pooling arrangement with BH Finance LLC (Berkshire subsidiary): $44.6M receivable at end of 2024
Capital expenditures modest ($4.9M in 2024) — asset-light franchisor model

tables.title: Training Program Summary
rows: Classroom in Minneapolis, MN or designated location
table_notes: Phase 1: ~2.5 weeks; includes restaurant operations 116 hrs, customer service 3 hrs, sanitation 2 hrs, safety 2 hrs
Phase 2: ~2.5 weeks; includes restaurant operations 104 hrs, financial management 8 hrs, marketing 2 hrs, register/back office 8 hrs
Phase 3: 4 days classroom; includes facility management 3 hrs, HR 7 hrs, customer service 4 hrs, situational leadership 8 hrs, goals/change 4 hrs, profitability 6 hrs
ARD locations may test out of Phase 1 and/or Phase 2 with 12+ months DQ management experience
Must complete Phase 3 within 6 months of Phase 2 or pay additional $1,000/person

tables.title: Advertising/Marketing Fund Spending Breakdown (2024)
rows: Other (audits, CMN, POS, research, advisory councils)
table_notes: Based on combined sales promotion payments from all DQ business types
Percentages vary by individual restaurant, area, group, or type of DQ business
ADQ management fee: 7% of sales promotion payments received (not taken above 3% of gross sales)
